items.headline: India begins first caste census in 94 years as part of nationwide population count
items.body: India launched its census on April 1, counting 1.4 billion people and for the first time since 1931 enumerating caste categories beyond scheduled groups. The data will reshape political representation and welfare targeting across the subcontinent for decades.

items.headline: Cyclone flooding drives cholera cases above 200,000 across six southern African countries
items.body: Mozambique, Malawi, Zimbabwe, Zambia, Tanzania, and South Africa report surging cholera infections following Cyclone Filipo's March flooding, with treatment centres overwhelmed. The outbreak threatens regional food security as farmers cannot access fields during planting season.

items.headline: Philippines extends energy emergency as power rationing enters fifth week
items.body: Rotating blackouts continue across Luzon affecting 60 million people after the government extended emergency powers through April to address coal and natural gas shortages. Manufacturing output has contracted as factories operate partial shifts and hospitals rely on backup generators.

items.headline: France deploys additional troops to New Caledonia after independence referendum violence
items.body: Paris sent 300 more security personnel to the Pacific territory this week following clashes between pro-independence groups and loyalists ahead of a May self-determination vote. The deployment signals France's concern about losing its largest exclusive economic zone and Indo-Pacific strategic position.
